questions.question: What quote style does the user prefer for Python strings?

questions.question: What logging approach does the user require instead of print()?
questions.answer: Python's logging module — logger.info(), logger.error(), etc.

questions.question: What ORM does the project use?
questions.notes: Corrected from raw SQL in session 2

questions.question: What rule does the user have about reading code before writing?
questions.answer: Always read existing code before writing new code to avoid duplicating existing patterns

questions.question: Does the project use Peewee for any database operations?
questions.answer: No, only SQLAlchemy. The user explicitly rejected Peewee and requires one ORM only.
questions.notes: Peewee was briefly used in session 2 before being corrected to SQLAlchemy only

questions.question: Does the project use raw SQL queries?
questions.answer: No, the user corrected this — all database access must use SQLAlchemy
questions.notes: Raw SQL used initially in session 2, corrected to SQLAlchemy

questions.question: Is it acceptable to use print() for logging in this project?
questions.answer: No, print() was explicitly banned in favor of the logging module

questions.question: Should docstrings be added to all functions?
questions.answer: No, only add comments where logic isn't self-evident. Don't add docstrings to functions the user didn't ask to document.

questions.question: What coding preference has the user had to correct more than once?
questions.answer: Single quotes — the assistant reverted to double quotes in session 2 after being told in session 1
questions.notes: Corrected in session 1, assistant reverted in session 2, corrected again

questions.question: What lesson was learned about reading existing code?
questions.answer: The assistant duplicated a validation pattern that already existed in marshmallow schemas because it didn't read the existing code first
